svg.title: ElectroCookie PCB placement on breadboard nodes
svg.desc: Component placement on the ElectroCookie solderable breadboard: power-rail buses on the long edges, 5-hole node columns split by a center gap, with the MT3608 and ESP32-S3 straddling the gap and the 4-channel TVS/output section at the right.
svg.text: PCB placement — following the breadboard nodes
Each vertical 5-hole group = one trace (node). ICs straddle the center gap; the edge rails carry +5V and GND.
on female headers, straddling the gap
Rails (long edges): top = +5V, bottom = GND. Jumper each part's 5V/GND pin straight to the nearest rail.
MT3608 (left): straddles the gap, each pad on its own node. IN+ from the 7.4V terminal; OUT+ jumpered to the +5V rail. Trim to 5.0V first.
ESP32-S3 (center): on two 8-pin female header strips across the gap — every pin gets its own node + 4 free holes for jumpers.
Signal: GPIO4-7 jumper to the relay-signal terminal (6-wire ribbon); GPIO2 → R1 → status-LED lead.
Outputs (right): per channel, relay NO lands on a node; a TVS bridges that node to the GND rail (cathode to +); a terminal taps it out to the post.
7.4V has no dedicated rail here — it rides a terminal + jumpers. If your board has a 2nd rail per side, dedicate it to +7.4V instead.
